OXA-51-like are not carbapenemases, with a few exceptions: carbapenem-resistance mediated through the OXA-51-like need two things; ISAba1 upstream, and a valine at position 167. evidence limitations: lacks evidence of the degree to which MIC is affected
rule curation note: OXA-51-like are wt carbapenem-susceptible. OXA-51-like are not carbapenemases, with a few exceptions: carbapenem-resistance mediated through the OXA-51-like need two things; ISAba1 upstream, and a valine at position 167. This valine is found in OXA-82, OXA-107, OXA-113, and OXA-219.(also OXA-108,-115,-138,-174,-175,-176,-177,-194,-195,-195,-197,-219,-545). In a chromosomal context, OXA-66 not preceded by ISAba1 increases the MIC of imipenem from 0.5 mg/L to 2 mg/L, and when OXA-66 is preceded by ISAba1 it increases the MIC of imipenem from 0.5 mg/L to 4 mg/L, in both cases not reaching the resitance level (PMID19307373).
The presence of OXA-51, OXA-66 or OXA-99 increases the MIC of imipenem from 0.125 mg/L to 1 mg/L (PMID 35562886).

Core gene encoding aspermidine and spermine efflux pump (PMID: 34552198). Relevance to antimicrobials lacks evidence.

Fluoroquinolone resistance: FQ-R is mediated by amino acid substitutions in GyrA and ParC, the vast majority of FQ-R isolates have the following substitutions, S81L and S84L, in GyrA and ParC respectively. There are a few other substitutions in the literature. Be aware, that earlier publications used E. coli numbering for GyrA/ParC. Nowadays we don’t use the E. coli numbering. wild type GyrA isolates are susceptible to FQ's

evidence code: ECO:0001103 natural variation mutant evidence, ECO:0001113 point mutation phenotypic evidence
rule curation note: Fluoroquinolone resistance: FQ-R is mediated by amino acid substitutions in GyrA and ParC, the vast majority of FQ-R isolates have the following substitutions, S81L and S84L, in GyrA and ParC respectively. There are a few other substitutions in the literature. Be aware, that earlier publications used E. coli numbering for GyrA/ParC. Nowadays we don’t use the E. coli numbering. Ser81-Leu GyrA isolates are resistant to FQ's with ciprofloxacin MICs raised to 4mg/L
AMG's: ant(3'')-IIa and ant(3'')-IIc are intrinsic (>99% of genomes). These enzymes confer resistance only to spectinomycin and streptomycin.

AMG's: ant(3'')-IIa and ant(3'')-IIc are intrinsic (>99% of genomes). These enzymes confer resistance only to spectinomycin and streptomycin. When expressed in E. coli it increases MIC for spectinomycin from 32 to 1024-2048 mg/L.

AMG's: ant(3'')-IIa and ant(3'')-IIc are intrinsic (>99% of genomes). These enzymes confer resistance only to spectinomycin and streptomycin. When expressed in E. coli it increases MIC for streptomycin from 4 to 128-512 mg/L.

OXA-51-like beta-lactamases can be also present in plasmids, when they are generally preceded by ISAba1. Without ISAba1, OXA-82 in a plasmid is not able to induce carbapenem resistance, the MIC for imipenem was increased from 0.5 mg/L to 2 ml/L (PMID 20713680).

evidence code: ECO:0001103 natural variation mutant evidence, ECO:0000005 enzymatic activity assay evidence
evidence limitations: lacks evidence of the degree to which MIC is affected
rule curation note: OXA-51-like beta-lactamases can be also present in plasmids, when they are generally preceded by ISAba1. Without ISAba1, OXA-82 in a plasmid is not able to induce carbapenem resistance, the MIC for imipenem was increased from 0.5 mg/L to 2 ml/L (PMID 20713680).

OXA-82, when present in a plasmid, is able to induce carbapenem resistance by increasing the MIC of  imipenem from 0.5 mg/L to 32 mg/L (PMID 20713680). Similarly, in the same conditions, OXA-138 and OXA-194 (both also containing the L167V mutation) increased the MIC of imipenem from 0.5 mg/L to 64 mg/L and 8 mg/L, respectively (PMID 22083478).

Fluoroquinolone resistance: FQ-R is mediated by amino acid substitutions in GyrA and ParC, the vast majority of FQ-R isolates have the following substitutions, S81L and S84L, in GyrA and ParC respectively. There are a few other substitutions in the literature. Be aware, that earlier publications used E. coli numbering for GyrA/ParC. Nowadays we don’t use the E. coli numbering. wild type GyrA isolates are susceptible to FQ's

evidence code: ECO:0000012 functional complementation evidence, ECO:0001091 knockout phenotypic evidence
rule curation note: Deletion of this gene lowered the MIC for spectinomycin from 5120 to 2560 mg/L. Complementation restored the original level of resistance.

evidence code: ECO:0000012 functional complementation evidence, ECO:0001091 knockout phenotypic evidence
rule curation note: Deletion of this gene lowered the MIC for streptomycin from 1024 to 64 mg/L. Complementation restored the original level of resistance.

evidence code: ECO:0001091 knockout phenotypic evidence, ECO:0000012 functional complementation evidence
rule curation note: AbaF is an MFS efflux pump. Gene knockout in A. baumannii revealed its contribution to fosfomycin resistance. No breakpoint or ECOFF values are available for fosfomycin in A. bamumannii.

evidence code: ECO:0001091 knockout phenotypic evidence, ECO:0000012 functional complementation evidence
rule curation note: When abaF is disrupted in A. baumannii, the MIC for fosfomycin decreases from 256 mg/L to 32 mg/L. Complementation of the gene restored the resistance.

evidence code: ECO:0001091 knockout phenotypic evidence, ECO:0000012 functional complementation evidence
rule curation note: Gene knockout lowered the MIC from >256 to 2 mg/L
